title: alopecurus palustris subsp. fulvus
intro: Alopecurus palustris subsp. fulvus, also known as yellow foxtail grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is known to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Alopecurus palustris subsp. fulvus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
